title: Fimbristylis atrosanguinea
taxonomy: Fimbristylis atrosanguinea belongs to the family Cyperaceae, which includes a diverse group of plants commonly known as sedges and rushes. This family consists of over 5,000 species and is found in various habitats around the world. Fimbristylis atrosanguinea is further classified within the genus Fimbristylis, a group of plants characterized by their distinctive leaf and flower structure. The species is also classified as a flowering plant, with its flowers typically arranged in a spike-like inflorescence. Further classification of Fimbristylis atrosanguinea can be made using various taxonomic keys and identification guides.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. intro: Fimbristylis atrosanguinea, also known as black-flowered fimbri-stylis or black fimbri-stylis, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the Cyperaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to treat various ailments such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Fimbristylis atrosanguinea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asian cultures, where it is valued for its ability to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
